clusters.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
clusters.neutral_headline: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC Top Stories, France24 and others

items.title: Details emerge of Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding events at MSG
items.standfirst: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.
items.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans include a rehearsal dinner and a late-night celebration at Madison Square Garden in New York City, according to sources familiar with the security planning.

items.title: Sabrina Carpenter and Graham Norton spotted in NYC before rumoured Swift wedding celebrations
items.standfirst: Stars have been spotted arriving in New York ahead of Taylor Swift's rumoured wedding celebrations at Madison Square Garden.
items.content: Stars have been spotted arriving in New York ahead of Taylor Swift's rumoured wedding celebrations at Madison Square Garden.

items.title: Celeb planners on how MSG could become Taylor Swift's perfect wedding venue
items.standfirst: Two elite event designers shared their thoughts on how Taylor Swift's team might transform Madison Square Garden.
items.content: Two elite event designers shared their thoughts on how Taylor Swift's team might transform Madison Square Garden.

items.title: New York City buzzes with expectation of Taylor Swift's rumoured July 3 wedding
items.standfirst: New York City is bracing for a very busy 4th of July weekend as World Cup matches mix with huge Independence Day celebrations for America's 250th anniversary and apparently popstar Taylor Swift's wedding to football player Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden. Information has been leaking out, with reports of a rehearsal dinner followed by a larger celebration in front of 1,000 people. The big event also coincides with a massive heatwave that has prompted city officials to declare a heat emergency.
items.content: New York City is bracing for a very busy 4th of July weekend as World Cup matches mix with huge Independence Day celebrations for America's 250th anniversary and apparently popstar Taylor Swift's wedding to football player Travis Kelce at Madison Square Garden. Information has been leaking out, with reports of a rehearsal dinner followed by a larger celebration in front of 1,000 people. The big event also coincides with a massive heatwave that has prompted city officials to declare a heat emergency.

items.title: From friendship bracelets to wedding rings: A Taylor and Travis timeline
items.standfirst: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.
items.content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

items.title: Taylor Swift's history with MSG, from talent competition to wedding bash
items.standfirst: Taylor Swift has had a decadeslong history with Madison Square Garden, performing there as early as 2003 and even celebrating her 30th birthday at the venue.
items.content: Taylor Swift has had a decadeslong history with Madison Square Garden, performing there as early as 2003 and even celebrating her 30th birthday at the venue.

clusters.title: Nine Buddhist monks killed by child driving truck in Thailand
clusters.neutral_headline: Nine Buddhist monks killed by child driving truck in Thailand
bullet_summary: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck into a group of monks on a pilgrimage walk in northeastern Thailand on Thursday, killing nine, officials said
Reported by Al Jazeera, Guardian World, BBC World and others

items.title: Nine Buddhist monks killed by child driving truck in Thailand
items.standfirst: Nine Buddhist monks were killed after a truck driven by an 11-year-old boy struck a pilgrimage procession in Thailand.
items.content: Nine Buddhist monks were killed after a truck driven by an 11-year-old boy struck a pilgrimage procession in Thailand.

items.title: At least nine monks killed in Thailand after boy drives truck into procession
items.standfirst: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstancesAn 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks.CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...
items.content: Charges yet to be filed over incident in Mukdahan involving 11-year-old, as police seek to establish circumstancesAn 11-year-old boy has driven his parents’ truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing at least nine monks.CCTV footage shared by a local rescue group showed the moment the monks, wearing orange robes, were run over as they walked in procession along a road. The timestamp on the footage was shortly before 11am local time on Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: Nine Thai monks killed after 11-year-old driver collides with procession
items.standfirst: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.
items.content: The group of 35 monks and five lay followers were walking by the roadside when the truck ploughed into them.

items.content: An 11-year-old boy crashed a pickup truck into a group of monks on a pilgrimage walk in northeastern Thailand on Thursday, killing nine, officials said.

clusters.title: Protests turn violent in Albania over development backed by Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner
clusters.neutral_headline: Violent clashes as Albania’s anti-government protests continue
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Al Jazeera, Sky News World

items.title: Protests turn violent in Albania over development backed by Ivanka Trump and Jared Kushner
items.standfirst: Anti-government protests turn violent in Albania as demonstrators call for the prime minister to step down after his government greenlit a plan backed by members of the Trump family to transform protected shorelines into luxury real estate. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports.
items.content: Anti-government protests turn violent in Albania as demonstrators call for the prime minister to step down after his government greenlit a plan backed by members of the Trump family to transform protected shorelines into luxury real estate. NBC News' Molly Hunter reports.

items.content: Police in Albania have clashed with protesters during an anti-government demonstration linked to a luxury resort connected to Donald Trump's son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

clusters.title: CPA examines Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income

bullet_summary: President Trump's recent financial disclosure showed he made $1
Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: CPA examines Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income
items.standfirst: President Trump's recent financial disclosure showed he made $1.4 billion off his cryptocurrency ventures in 2025. Shehan Chandrasekera, CPA and head of tax strategy at CoinTracker, joins with analysis.
items.content: President Trump's recent financial disclosure showed he made $1.4 billion off his cryptocurrency ventures in 2025. Shehan Chandrasekera, CPA and head of tax strategy at CoinTracker, joins with analysis.

items.title: New questions about Trump's taxes after financial disclosure release
items.standfirst: President Trump's financial disclosure is raising many questions. For some, these include ethical concerns about whether he is profiting from the presidency. It's also highlighting another mystery: how much is he paying in taxes? CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has more.
items.content: President Trump's financial disclosure is raising many questions. For some, these include ethical concerns about whether he is profiting from the presidency. It's also highlighting another mystery: how much is he paying in taxes? CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has more.

items.title: Taxes on Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income could total hundreds of millions, sources say
items.standfirst: President Trump made $1.4 billion through his crypto ventures in 2025, according to a new financial disclosure. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has reporting on the potential tax payments the president may have to make based on those profits.
items.content: President Trump made $1.4 billion through his crypto ventures in 2025, according to a new financial disclosure. CBS News senior White House correspondent Weijia Jiang has reporting on the potential tax payments the president may have to make based on those profits.

items.title: Taxes on Trump's $1.4 billion crypto income could total hundreds of millions
items.standfirst: The White House declined to say how much President Trump paid in taxes on his crypto windfall.
items.content: The White House declined to say how much President Trump paid in taxes on his crypto windfall.

items.title: From Truman's pension to Trump's billions - a White House windfall unmatched by any president

items.content: Historians say Trump's $2.2bn income last year is unprecedented and blurs the line on conflicts of interest.

clusters.title: Details on Olympian's felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
clusters.neutral_headline: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Details on Olympian's felony charge over alleged Reflecting Pool vandalism
items.standfirst: U.S. Olympian David Hearn is facing a felony charge after being accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. CBS News senior Justice Department reporter Sarah Lynch has the details.
items.content: U.S. Olympian David Hearn is facing a felony charge after being accused of vandalizing the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. CBS News senior Justice Department reporter Sarah Lynch has the details.

items.title: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly tearing up part of Reflecting Pool
items.standfirst: Former U.S. Olympian David Hearn was indicted on Thursday on a felony charge for allegedly tearing up roughly two feet of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool last month, amid the algae blooms and political mudslinging around the president's $16 million renovation. Nicole Sganga reports.
items.content: Former U.S. Olympian David Hearn was indicted on Thursday on a felony charge for allegedly tearing up roughly two feet of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool last month, amid the algae blooms and political mudslinging around the president's $16 million renovation. Nicole Sganga reports.

items.title: Former Olympian indicted for allegedly vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool

clusters.title: AOC endorses progressive Democrat in closely watched Michigan race for US Senate seat
clusters.neutral_headline: AOC endorses Abdul El-Sayed in Michigan Senate race
bullet_summary: “Despite our ideological differences and whatever disagreements there are in the party, every single one of us sees this moment as existential,” she said
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: AOC endorses progressive Democrat in closely watched Michigan race for US Senate seat
items.standfirst: Abdul El-Sayed, backed by Bernie Sanders, leads polls ahead of Haley Stevens and Mallory McMorrow in primaryAlexandria Ocasio-Cortez has thrown her support behind Abdul El-Sayed, the doctor and progressive Democrat seeking the party’s nomination in Michigan’s closely watched US Senate race.In an interview with the New York Times, Ocasio-Cortez – an influential congresswoman on the left of the Democratic party – endorsed El-Sayed, a former public health director. “Despite our ideological differences and whatever disagreements there are in the party, every single one of us sees this moment as existential,” she said. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: AOC endorses Abdul El-Sayed in Michigan Senate race
items.standfirst: Michigan progressive Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed has scored an endorsement from Democratic Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York, her first in a contested Senate primary for the 2026 cycle. Political strategists Joel Payne and Sarah Chamberlain join with analysis.
items.content: Michigan progressive Senate candidate Abdul El-Sayed has scored an endorsement from Democratic Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York, her first in a contested Senate primary for the 2026 cycle. Political strategists Joel Payne and Sarah Chamberlain join with analysis.

clusters.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
clusters.neutral_headline: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
bullet_summary: Reported by NBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Mexico scene shows crowds that killed three during World Cup celebration
items.standfirst: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.
items.content: Two women and one man died of asphyxiation in downtown Mexico City as thousands of soccer fans celebrated their national team’s World Cup win over Ecuador.

items.title: Four people die in mass fan celebrations in Mexico City after World Cup victory
items.standfirst: Three of the victims died from suffocationMore than a million people gathered in Mexico CityFour people died, three ⁠from suffocation, as thousands of fans crowded Mexico City streets during World Cup celebrations, the capital’s health secretariat ⁠said in the early ⁠hours ​of Wednesday.The deaths occurred near the Angel of Independence landmark, where thousands of fans had gathered to ⁠celebrate Mexico’s 2-0 victory over Ecuador in the last 32. Continue reading...
